selected_goal: Detect duplicate question and mark-scheme image-path mappings in the current output-integrity audit.
why_this_now: After iteration 1 added suspicious crop detection, the next highest-value integrity risk from the backlog is duplicate mappings where multiple records unexpectedly point to the same canonical rendered asset.
repo_evidence: .agent-loop/BACKLOG.md includes duplicate mappings where multiple records point to the same canonical question or mark-scheme image unexpectedly.
src/exam_bank/audit.py already gathers per-record question and mark-scheme path entries but only validates existence and absoluteness.
tests/test_audit.py already has current-output-integrity fixtures that can express shared image paths without generating new fixture trees.
acceptance_criteria: The audit fails when two or more records reference the same nonblank question image path.
The audit fails when two or more records reference the same nonblank mark-scheme image path.
Repeated path fields inside the same record, such as question_image_path plus canonical_question_artifact, are deduplicated and do not create false duplicate mapping failures.
test_expectations: Add or update tests in tests/test_audit.py to prove cross-record duplicate question and mark-scheme image paths are failures.
implementation_boundaries: Do not rewrite output files or asset manifests.
Do not infer legitimate shared images; report duplicate mappings as review-required failures.
Keep duplicate detection path-based and deterministic.
repo_hygiene_risk: Low. The change extends an existing audit and existing tests only.
rollback_plan: Remove the duplicate path collection/checks and the related test assertions.
done_when: Targeted tests pass and a live output-integrity audit records whether current output has duplicate image path mappings.
